topics.name: Rehabilitation
topics.description: The passage discusses a person, group, or work that was historically ignored, undervalued, or misunderstood, with the author's goal being to correct the record.

topics.name: Phenomenon
topics.description: The passage introduces a natural puzzle (a phenomenon) and discusses hypotheses to explain it, focusing on mechanisms rather than opinions.

topics.name: Critique
topics.description: The passage analyzes a broad system (often Law or Economics), identifying a major flaw or proposing a reform.

structures.name: Debate
structures.description: The passage centers on a conflict between two opposing viewpoints (A vs. B), with the author usually picking a side or synthesizing them.

structures.name: Explanation
structures.description: There is no central conflict; instead, a mystery or question is presented followed by a factual explanation (Mystery -> Solution).

structures.name: Thesis
structures.description: The author presents a single strong opinion or descriptive account and piles on evidence to support it without a significant opponent.

difficulty.name: Low (Easy)
difficulty.description: Clear main point, standard vocabulary, distinct viewpoints. Accuracy >75%.
difficulty.description_cn: 原文直接提取，结构透明。全球正确率 >75%。

difficulty.name: Medium
difficulty.description: Standard LSAT density, some technical jargon, subtle author opinion. Accuracy 40%-75%.
difficulty.description_cn: 涉及信息综合或次级推理。正确率 40%-75%。

difficulty.name: High (Hard)
difficulty.description: Abstract philosophy/science, non-linear structure, nuanced answer choices. Accuracy <40%.
difficulty.description_cn: 抽象主题，密集长难句或强干扰项。正确率 <40%。

task_codes.name: Structure
task_codes.description: Describe method, find main point, parallel reasoning.

task_codes.name: Necessary
task_codes.description: Requirement that must be true (The Shield).

task_codes.name: Inference
task_codes.description: Must be true, most strongly supported, no argument.

detailed_task_codes.name: Strengthen
detailed_task_codes.description: Add a premise that makes the conclusion more likely to be true.

detailed_task_codes.name: Weaken
detailed_task_codes.description: Add a premise that makes the conclusion less likely to be true.

detailed_task_codes.name: Sufficient Assumption
detailed_task_codes.description: Add a premise that logically guarantees the conclusion (100% validity).

detailed_task_codes.name: Necessary Assumption
detailed_task_codes.description: Identify a requirement that must be true for the argument to hold.

detailed_task_codes.name: Flaw
detailed_task_codes.description: Abstractly describe the logical error in the reasoning.

detailed_task_codes.name: Parallel Flaw
detailed_task_codes.description: Identify an answer choice that commits the exact same logical error as the stimulus.
indicators: Most similar to the flawed reasoning

detailed_task_codes.name: Parallel Reasoning
detailed_task_codes.description: Match the logical structure of a valid or neutral argument.
indicators: Pattern of reasoning is most similar to

detailed_task_codes.name: Main Point
detailed_task_codes.description: Identify the main conclusion of the argument.

detailed_task_codes.name: Role
detailed_task_codes.description: Describe the specific function of a single statement within the argument.
indicators: Plays which one of the following roles
The reference to X serves to

detailed_task_codes.name: Method
detailed_task_codes.description: Describe the abstract argumentative technique or strategy used in the entire argument.

detailed_task_codes.name: Point at Issue
detailed_task_codes.description: Identify the specific statement that two speakers disagree on.

detailed_task_codes.name: Inference
detailed_task_codes.description: Identify what must be true based strictly on the facts provided.

detailed_task_codes.name: Resolve Paradox
detailed_task_codes.description: Explain how two apparently conflicting facts can both be true.

engine_codes.name: Deductive
engine_codes.description: Formal logic, absolute rules, certain conclusion.

engine_codes.name: Inductive
engine_codes.description: General evidence supporting a probable conclusion.

engine_codes.name: No Argument
engine_codes.description: Stimulus contains no argument (fact set only).

difficulty.name: Level 1 (Warm-Up)
difficulty.description: Very Easy. Simple vocabulary; explicit conclusion.
difficulty.description_cn: 非常简单。词汇基础，结论明确。

difficulty.name: Level 2 (Easy)
difficulty.description: Easy. Standard argument structure; one clear gap.
difficulty.description_cn: 简单。标准论证结构，存在单一明显逻辑漏洞。

difficulty.name: Level 3 (Standard-Low)
difficulty.description: Standard - Low. Correct answer fits prediction perfectly.
difficulty.description_cn: 标准偏易。正确选项与预判完全吻合。

difficulty.name: Level 4 (Standard)
difficulty.description_cn: 标准。典型的 LSAT 信息密度。

difficulty.name: Level 5 (Moderate)
difficulty.description_cn: 标准中等。选项包含陷阱（Shell Game）。

difficulty.name: Level 6 (Standard-High)
difficulty.description: Standard - High. Abstract language for simple concepts.
difficulty.description_cn: 标准偏难。用抽象语言描述简单概念。

difficulty.name: Level 7 (Hard)
difficulty.description_cn: 困难。涉及多步逻辑推导。

difficulty.name: Level 8 (Very Hard)
difficulty.description: Very Hard. Dense philosophical or scientific topic.
difficulty.description_cn: 非常困难。密集哲学或科学类主题。

difficulty.name: Level 9 (The Killer)
difficulty.description: The Killer. Formal logic with Most/Some quantifiers.
difficulty.description_cn: 杀手级。包含 Most/Some 量词的严密形式逻辑。

difficulty.name: Level 10 (Legendary)
difficulty.description: Legendary. Logic turns on a single semantic shift.
difficulty.description_cn: 传奇级。逻辑取决于细微的语义转变。
